code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test30-JUN-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings are consistent with renal dysfunction, possibly diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
